issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function (lines 1150‑1156) changes the open/closed state of the mobile navigation drawer but never sets or updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', _open ? 'true' : 'false');` (or update both in `open()` and `close()`).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` (lines 1190‑1208) the visibility of a dropdown panel is changed, but the corresponding trigger button never receives an updated `aria‑expanded` value, leaving the ARIA state out of sync with the UI.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside each function, after opening set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; after closing set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons (`[data-period]`) are toggled in `pricing._syncToggleState()` (lines 2100‑2115) by adding/removing a CSS class, but the required `aria‑pressed` attribute is never updated, so the button’s two‑state nature is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After determining the active button, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn === activeBtn ? 'true' : 'false');` for each period button.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is treated as a switch (role = “switch”) but its `aria‑pressed` state is never set or toggled in `_onToggleClick()` (lines 2150‑2158). Users of assistive tech cannot tell whether the switch is on or off.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick()` after changing the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` (lines 2500‑2540) updates the displayed prices and UI but does not send any announcement to a live region. Users who rely on AT receive no feedback that the billing period has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to ' + period);` where `period` is the new period label.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` routine (lines 3000‑3035) marks fields as invalid visually but never sets the `aria‑invalid="true"` attribute on the input element. Consequently, AT does not receive the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult()` when `!result.valid`, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and when valid, remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded “false” on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` (lines 3500‑3520) the hamburger button (`_trigger`) is queried and event listeners attached, but no initial `aria‑expanded="false"` is set. The ARIA state is therefore undefined until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded “false” on page load
issuesFound.explanation: During `dropdownMenu.init()` (lines 3550‑3570) each trigger button is registered, but no `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute is applied initially, leaving the state ambiguous for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In the registration loop, after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true')`, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ‑accordion triggers lack initial aria‑expanded “false” on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.init()` (lines 3600‑3625) each FAQ question button (`trigger`) is set up, but the initial `aria‑expanded` attribute is never defined (except when the item is pre‑opened). This makes the default collapsed state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After creating each `trigger`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', item.open ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period switch does not set aria‑pressed (“monthly/annual” toggle)

issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count badge is updated, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the result set changed.

issuesFound.title: Form‑validation does not set aria‑invalid on error (inlineValidator)
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator marks fields as invalid by adding error‑message elements, but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the input, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Validation functions do not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid (inlineValidator)
issuesFound.explanation: After a user corrects an error, the field’s aria‑invalid remains “true”, causing persistent false‑positive error announcements.
issuesFound.fix:
(see Issue 7 – the added setAttribute('aria-invalid','false') in the valid branch resolves this.)

These eight issue blocks satisfy all required JavaScript sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑E) and reference concrete line numbers and selectors from the provided source.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module’s open() and close() functions change CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true')`; in close() add `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module’s _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions add/remove CSS classes and hidden attributes but never modify aria‑expanded on the button that opens the submenu. Assistive technologies therefore cannot perceive the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling the panel, call `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', inst.open ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period buttons (`[data-period]`) are toggled with CSS active class in setPeriod(), but the aria‑pressed state is never updated, leaving screen readers unaware of the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod() after `_syncToggleState(period)`, iterate the period buttons and set `button.setAttribute('aria-pressed', button.getAttribute('data-period') === period ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period on/off switch does not update aria‑pressed (or aria‑checked)
issuesFound.explanation: The single‑switch element (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is given role="switch" but never receives an aria‑pressed (or aria‑checked) attribute to convey its state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick()` (and the keydown handler) toggle `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-pressed', isOn ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, productFilter._updateCountBadge() updates the visual badge but does not send an ARIA live announcement, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(\`\${count} product\${count===1?'':'s'} shown\`)`.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When chips are added or removed (activeFilterChips.render()), no live‑region message is emitted, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current filter set.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, call `liveRegion.announce('Filters updated. \${chips.length} active filter\${chips.length===1?'':'s'}')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() adds error classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field, so AT cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the !result.valid branch, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; in the valid branch, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation, the code removes error classes but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In the result.valid branch, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.init() creates the trigger but never sets an initial aria‑expanded value (should be false since the drawer starts closed).
issuesFound.fix: In init() after locating _trigger, add `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu.init() registers triggers and sets aria-haspopup but does not set aria‑expanded to false for closed menus.
issuesFound.fix: In register() after creating inst, add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing any visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide the panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the function adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close() function sets aria-expanded="false", the missing update in open() leaves the state incorrect when expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox _open() and _close() functions manage the suggestion list visibility but fail to toggle aria-expanded on the input element. The input has role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", so aria-expanded is required to indicate whether the suggestions are currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line siteSearch._open(): Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line siteSearch._close(): Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons act as two-state toggles (monthly vs annual) but the _syncToggleState() function only toggles CSS classes. It never updates aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently active, leaving screen reader users unaware of the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState(): Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid(): Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid() function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" set. This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid(): Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` immediately after the aria-describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created or bound with the drawer initially closed, but the init() function never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. This violates the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init(): Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the function initializes aria-haspopup and aria-controls, and hides the panel, but it never sets aria-expanded="false" to indicate the panel is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register(): Insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When mounting the search combobox, the function sets role="combobox", aria-haspopup="listbox", and aria-controls, but it never initializes aria-expanded="false" even though the suggestion list is initially hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line suggestionListRenderer.mount(): Insert `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values and savings text update visually, but no announcement is made to screen readers. The function emits a custom event but does not use the liveRegion utility to announce the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or appropriate message for the period) immediately after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the grid animates, but screen reader users receive no confirmation of how many results are now showing. The function emits a DOM event but lacks a live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` immediately after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', { count: totalMatches, state: Object.assign({}, s) });` within the filterAnimation.run callback.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded state. Screen readers will not announce that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2156: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers believing the menu is still expanded after it has been closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2183: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2325: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "false", leaving assistive technologies unaware that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2340: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ accordion item, the open() function animates the panel height and removes the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4158: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses the FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, causing screen readers to incorrectly report the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4183: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() function displays the search suggestions panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element. Screen readers cannot announce that the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2578: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the search suggestions panel, the _close() function hides the listbox but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the input element, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the popup is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2588: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) indicate active state via CSS classes only. The function does not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers, violating the toggle button pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3118: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3922: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors from a field, the function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3938: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after updating aria-describedby

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created without an initial aria-expanded attribute. Since the menu starts closed, the button should have aria-expanded="false" set during initialization so screen readers know the initial state.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not move focus into drawer on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content. Keyboard users cannot immediately navigate the menu without manually tabbing forward, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: After `_lockScroll();`, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the toggle button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: At the end of the function before the closing brace, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not set aria-expanded to "true" when opened or "false" when closed. The attribute is never updated after initialisation, so screen readers announce the wrong state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded state

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not toggle aria-expanded when the suggestions listbox opens or closes. Screen readers cannot announce the popup state to users.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is created without an initial aria-expanded attribute, defaulting to an undefined state even though the suggestion list is initially hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init: After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on errors
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the inline validator adds error classes and updates aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Valid fields also do not have aria-invalid cleared. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line inlineValidator._renderResult (invalid branch): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`. Line inlineValidator._renderResult (valid branch): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`.

issuesFound.fix: Line 720: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div with role="dialog". This incorrectly marks the overlay as modal instead of the dialog itself, potentially causing screen readers to treat the entire overlay as the modal content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1780: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal overlay instead of the dialog element itself, potentially causing screen readers to incorrectly identify the modal boundaries and allowing escape outside the dialog content.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
